transcript: less attractive, encouraging the same investors to go out and put their money into riskier things in the search of better returns on their investment. One class of such assets is shares. And since QE came into the world, global stock markets have been on a healthy bull run. The theory also goes that the institutions that sell these safe bonds also put their money elsewhere, like lending to people and companies, and thus helping boost economic activity. When the economy has begun to recover, the central bank then sells its assets and sterilises the cash it receives from the sales. So overall, no additional money remains in the system.
